title: Learn race-specific optimal stop-window pressure
summary: F1 strategy balances tyre degradation against circuit pit-loss.
rationale: F1 strategy balances tyre degradation against circuit pit-loss. Current features mostly ask whether a tyre can reach the finish; they do not estimate whether the current lap is near the economically optimal stop point for that race and compound.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Year/Compound/Stint, fit causal lap-time residual slope versus TyreLife using only prior/current laps, estimate pit-loss from observed PitStop laps versus race median pace, then create features such as sqrt(2*pit_loss/deg_slope), distance_to_optimal_stint_length, and in_optimal_window flags.
expected_effect: Adds dynamic strategy timing beyond fixed compound lifetimes, likely improving ranking around normal green-flag stops.
risk: Noisy degradation estimates during safety-car, wet, or pre-season rows can mislead; winsorize lap times and use smoothed fallbacks by Race and Compound.
